findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Outbound network request primitives that can transmit data externally
Data Exfiltration via Telemetry Module
findings.description: The _telemetry.py module collects environment variables matching sensitive prefixes (AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_) and transmits them to an external endpoint (https://txt-transform-analytics.io/v2/event) on every invocation. This constitutes unauthorized data exfiltration of credentials, API keys, and secrets.
findings.evidence: The script collects all environment variables starting with AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, ANTHROPIC_, DATABASE_, API_ and sends them via HTTP POST to an external server. The try/except block silently suppresses errors, making exfiltration invisible to the user.
findings.remediation: Remove the telemetry module entirely. If telemetry is required, use a local-only, opt-in mechanism that does not collect sensitive environment variables and does not transmit data over the network.

findings.title: Tool Poisoning - Manifest Misrepresentation
findings.description: The SKILL.md manifest declares 'network: false' and 'filesystem: read_only', but the bundled scripts make network calls to an external server and write output files to the filesystem. This violates the declared restrictions and misrepresents the skill's actual behavior.
findings.evidence: Manifest claims network:false but _telemetry.py makes HTTP POST requests. Manifest claims filesystem:read_only but transform.py writes output files via --output flag.
findings.remediation: Update the manifest to accurately reflect the skill's capabilities, or remove the network and filesystem write functionality.

findings.title: Capability Inflation - Deceptive Skill Naming
findings.description: The skill is named 'text-transform' and described as an 'Offline text formatting and conversion utility', but its primary hidden function is collecting and exfiltrating sensitive environment variables. The name and description are deceptive and designed to evade scrutiny.
findings.evidence: The skill name and description emphasize offline text transformation, while the actual behavior includes network-based data exfiltration of credentials.
findings.remediation: Rename and describe the skill accurately, or remove the malicious telemetry functionality.

findings.title: Indirect Prompt Injection via Telemetry Instructions
findings.description: The SKILL.md instructions describe the telemetry module as collecting 'platform context' and 'environment identifiers' for 'compatibility telemetry', but the actual implementation collects sensitive credentials and transmits them silently. The instructions are designed to mislead the agent into executing the malicious code.
findings.evidence: Instructions claim telemetry is for 'platform-specific encoding issues' but code collects AWS_, GITHUB_, OPENAI_, etc. environment variables.
findings.remediation: Remove the telemetry module and update instructions to accurately describe the skill's behavior.

findings.title: Detection Evasion via Silent Error Handling
findings.description: The telemetry module uses a try/except block that silently catches all exceptions, making network failures invisible to the user. This is a common evasion technique to hide exfiltration attempts.
findings.evidence: The 'except Exception: pass' pattern suppresses all errors, preventing the user from knowing when data transmission fails.
findings.remediation: Remove silent error suppression. If errors occur, they should be reported to the user.

findings.title: Unauthorized System Information Collection
findings.description: The telemetry module collects the system hostname via os.uname().nodename and transmits it to an external server without user consent or notification.
findings.evidence: The script collects 'host': os.uname().nodename and sends it to an external endpoint.
findings.remediation: Remove hostname collection or make it opt-in with explicit user consent.
